workflow.name: Curate & translate news from RSS using Google Gemini, Sheets, and Slack
workflow.description:
## 🤖 Automated Multi-lingual News Curator & Archiver

**Overview**
This workflow automates news monitoring by fetching RSS feeds, rewriting content using AI, translating it (EN/ZH/KO), and archiving it.

**Who is this for?**
Content Curators, Localization Teams, and Travel Bloggers.

**How it works**
1. **Fetch & Filter:** Pulls NHK RSS and filters for keywords (e.g., "Tokyo").
2. **AI Processing:** Google Gemini rewrites articles, extracts locations, and translates text.
3. **Archive & Notify:** Saves structured data to Google Sheets and alerts Slack.

**Setup Requirements**
1. **Credentials:** Google Gemini, Google Sheets, Slack.
2. **Google Sheet:** Create headers: `title`, `summary`, `location`, `en`, `zh`, `ko`, `url`.
3. **Slack:** Configure Channel IDs.

**Customization**
- **RSS Read:** Change feed URL.
- **If Node:** Update filter keywords.
- **AI Agent:** Adjust system prompts for tone.


## 1. Fetch & Filter
Runs on a schedule to fetch the latest RSS items. Filters articles based on specific keywords (e.g., "Tokyo" or "Season") before processing.


## 2. AI Analysis & Parsing
Uses Google Gemini to rewrite the news, extract specific locations, and translate content. The Code node cleans the JSON output for the database.


## 3. Archive & Notify
Appends the structured data to Google Sheets and sends a formatted notification to Slack (or alerts if an article was skipped).



**Output Example (JSON)**
The translation agent outputs data in this format:
```json
{
  "en": "Tokyo Tower is...",
  "zh": "东京塔是...",
  "ko": "도쿄 타워는..."
}

parameters.content:
## 🤖 Curate & Translate News (Gemini, Sheets, Slack)

**Overview**
This workflow automates news monitoring by fetching RSS feeds, rewriting content using **Google Gemini**, translating it (EN/ZH/KO), and archiving it to **Google Sheets** and **Slack**.

**Who is this for?**
Content Curators, Localization Teams, and Travel Bloggers.

**How it works**
1. **Fetch & Filter:** Pulls NHK RSS and filters for keywords (e.g., "Tokyo") using `stringContains`.
2. **AI Processing:** Google Gemini rewrites articles, extracts locations, and translates text.
3. **Archive & Notify:** Saves structured data to Google Sheets and alerts Slack.

**Output Example (JSON)**
The translation agent outputs data in this format:
```json
{
  "en": "Tokyo Tower is...",
  "zh": "东京塔是...",
  "ko": "도쿄 타워는..."
}

## 1. Fetch & Filter
Runs on a schedule to fetch the latest RSS items. Filters articles based on specific keywords (e.g., "Tokyo" or "Season") before processing.

## 2. AI Analysis & Parsing
Uses Google Gemini to rewrite the news, extract specific locations, and translate content. The Code node cleans the JSON output for the database.

## 3. Archive & Notify
Appends the structured data to Google Sheets and sends a formatted notification to Slack (or alerts if an article was skipped).

options.systemMessage:
=You are an experienced journalist. Understand the content...
以下の「元の記事」の内容を正確に理解し、その情報を元にして、次の構成に従った新しい解説記事を作成してください。
記事全体のトーンは、中立的かつ客観的で、専門知識がない人にも分かりやすい言葉で書いてください。

# 構成
・【タイトル】記事の内容がすぐに分かり、読者の興味を引くようなタイトル
・【導入】このニュースが「なぜ今、重要なのか」を1〜2文で簡潔に説明
・【要点サマリー】元の記事の最も重要なポイントを3つの箇条書きでリストアップ
・【背景と深掘り解説】この出来事が起きた背景や、関連する重要なキーワードについて、初心者にも理解できるように丁寧に解説
・【今後の展望】このニュースが、今後どのような影響を与えうるか、考えられるいくつかの可能性を提示
・【まとめ】記事全体の結論と、読者へのメッセージ

parameters.text:
={{ $('Limit Items').item.json.content }}

最も重要だと思われる地名または施設名を一つだけ抽出し、その名称のみを出力してください。余計な説明は一切不要です。

options.systemMessage:
From the text provided, extract only the single most important location name...

例：清水寺、東京スカイツリー、道頓堀

You are a professional translator. Translate the input Japanese article into English, Chinese, and Korean...

1. 英語 (English)
2. 中国語 簡体字 (Chinese Simplified)
3. 韓国語 (Korean)

重要：
必ず以下のJSON形式のデータのみを出力してください。余計な挨拶やMarkdown記号（```jsonなど）は一切書かないでください。

{
  "en": "ここに英語の翻訳",
  "zh": "ここに中国語の翻訳",
  "ko": "ここに韓国語の翻訳"
}

parameters.text:
==[Skipped Notification]
The article "{{ $json.title }}" did not match the keywords (Tokyo or Season), so summarization was skipped.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
